coredata.dc:title: Ag-carbon dots with peroxidase-like activity for colorimetric and SERS dual mode detection of glucose and glutathione
coredata.dc:creator: Haiyang, Lv
Guantong, Liu
Nan, Zhang
Zhanye, Yang
Xinge, Jv
Bing, Zhao
Tian, Yuan
coredata.dc:description: Currently, nanozymes have made important research progress in the fields of catalysis, biosensing and tumor therapy, but most of nanozymes sensing systems are single-mode detection, which are easily affected by environment and operation, so it is crucial to construct nanozymes sensing system with dual-signal detection to obtain a more stable and reliable performance. In this paper, Ag-carbon dots (Ag-CDs) bifunctional nanomaterials were synthesized using carbon dots as reducing agent and protective agent by a facile and green one-step method. A simple and sensitive colorimetric-SERS dual-mode sensing platform was constructed for the detection of glucose and glutathione(GSH) in body fluids by taking advantage of good peroxidase-like and SERS activities of Ag-CDs. Ag-CDs catalyzes H2O2 to hydroxyl radicals(•OH), which oxidized TMB to form ox-TMB blue solution with characteristic absorption peak at 652 nm and Raman characteristic peak at 1607 cm−1. Ag-CDs sensing method exhibited high performance for glucose and GSH with detection limits for colorimetric and SERS as low as 11.30 μM and 3.54 μM, 0.38 μM and 0.24 μM respectively (S/N = 3). In addition, Ag-CDs have good stability and uniformity, ensuring long-term applicability of catalytic system. This colorimetric-SERS dual-mode sensing platform can be used for the determination of glucose and GSH in saliva and urine, and has the advantages of simple, low cost, rapid, and high accuracy, which has a potential application prospect in biosensor and medical research.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Dual-signal detection
Colorimetric-SERS
Ag-carbon dots
Nanozymes
Glucose
Glutathione

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Preparation of CDs and Ag NPs
POD-like enzyme activity and kinetic analysis
Detection of H2O2, glucose and GSH
Detection of glucose and GSH in real samples

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: The work was supported by Natural Science Foundation of Jilin Province of China (No.20220101067JC), the National Natural Science Foundation (Grant No.22011540378) of the P.R. China, the Development Program of the Science and Technology of Jilin Province (20210204217YY, 20220204111YY) and National Key Research and Development Program of China(2022YFA1504200).

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (a–b) TEM images of CDs、Ag-CDs; (c–d) HRTEM images of Ag-CDs; (e) statistical analysis of particle size distribution of Ag-CDs; (f) DLS image of Ag-CDs.

(a)UV–Vis spectra of CDs and Ag-CDs. Inset: Photographs of CDs and Ag-CDs solutions under visible light; (b)Fluorescence spectra of CDs and Ag-CDs. Inset: Photographs of CDs and Ag-CDs solutions under 365 nm UV light; (c) (d) (e) are XRD, FT-IR and Raman spectra of Ag-CDs, respectively; (f) Mechanism of fluorescence quenching of CDs in Ag-CDs.
XPS spectra of Ag-CDs (a) Full range spectra; (b) C 1s; (c) O 1s; (d) N 1s; (e) Ag 3d; (f) ESR spectra of •OH of different samples with DMPO as radical trapping agent.

(a)Glucose detection schematic; (b)UV–Vis spectra with different concentrations of glucose. Inset: photograph of the sample; (c) Relationship curve between absorbance of ox-TMB at 652 nm and glucose concentration. Inset: linear curve at 50–800 μM; (d)SERS spectra with different concentrations of glucose; (e) Relationship curve between characteristic peak intensity at 1607 cm−1 of ox-TMB and glucose concentration. Inset: linear curve from 10 to 800 μM.

(a)GSH detection schematic; UV–Vis spectra (b) with different concentrations of GSH. Inset: photograph of the sample; (c) Relationship curve between absorbance of ox-TMB at 652 nm and GSH concentration. Inset: linear curve from 1 to 60 μM; (d)SERS with different concentrations of GSH. (e) Relationship curve between characteristic peak intensity at 1607 cm−1 of ox-TMB and GSH concentration. Inset: linear curve from 1 to 70 μM.
Glucose test results in saliva and urine.

ce:table-footnote.ce:label: a
ce:table-footnote.ce:note-para: : Detected by method in this paper.

head.ce:title: Ag-carbon dots with peroxidase-like activity for colorimetric and SERS dual mode detection of glucose and glutathione

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Chemistry, Jilin Province Research Center for Engineering and Technology of Spectral Analytical Instruments, Jilin University, Changchun, 130012, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Jilin Province Research Center for Engineering and Technology of Spectral Analytical Instruments

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Chemistry, Jilin Province Research Center for Engineering and Technology of Spectral Analytical Instruments, Jilin University, Changchun, 130012, P. R. China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: State Key Laboratory of Supramolecular Structure and Materials, Jilin University, Changchun 130012, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: State Key Laboratory of Supramolecular Structure and Materials

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: State Key Laboratory of Supramolecular Structure and Materials, Jilin University, Changchun 130012, P. R. China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Currently, nanozymes have made important research progress in the fields of catalysis, biosensing and tumor therapy, but most of nanozymes sensing systems are single-mode detection, which are easily affected by environment and operation, so it is crucial to construct nanozymes sensing system with dual-signal detection to obtain a more stable and reliable performance. In this paper, Ag-carbon dots (Ag-CDs) bifunctional nanomaterials were synthesized using carbon dots as reducing agent and protective agent by a facile and green one-step method. A simple and sensitive colorimetric-SERS dual-mode sensing platform was constructed for the detection of glucose and glutathione(GSH) in body fluids by taking advantage of good peroxidase-like and SERS activities of Ag-CDs. Ag-CDs catalyzes H2O2 to hydroxyl radicals(•OH), which oxidized TMB to form ox-TMB blue solution with characteristic absorption peak at 652 nm and Raman characteristic peak at 1607 cm−1. Ag-CDs sensing method exhibited high performance for glucose and GSH with detection limits for colorimetric and SERS as low as 11.30 μM and 3.54 μM, 0.38 μM and 0.24 μM respectively (S/N = 3). In addition, Ag-CDs have good stability and uniformity, ensuring long-term applicability of catalytic system. This colorimetric-SERS dual-mode sensing platform can be used for the determination of glucose and GSH in saliva and urine, and has the advantages of simple, low cost, rapid, and high accuracy, which has a potential application prospect in biosensor and medical research.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Glucose and glutathione are important biomarkers that closely relate to individual health status, detecting these molecules in body fluids can be used to prevent, recognize and treat a wide range of diseases. Glucose is the most important source of energy in human body. Disturbances in glucose levels in body commonly cause a variety of diseases such as blindness, kidney failure and diabetes. Diabetes affects the health of nearly 10% of the world's population and also causes serious complications such as coma and epilepsy [1–4].GSH is an essential antioxidant with the ability to quench free radicals and plays a vital role in human, animal and plant life. However, disruption of GSH homeostasis may also contribute to a range of diseases, including cardiovascular diseases, cancer, aging, cystic fibrosis and excessive oxidative stress [5,6].Therefore, accurate and sensitive detection of glucose and GSH is important for biomedical research, clinical monitoring and diagnosis.

At present, several methods have been developed to detect glucose and GSH. Although those methods have their own advantages, like high sensitivity and fast detection speed, but they also have shortcomings such as operation complexity. For example, electrochemical method has high sensitivity, but requires complex modified electrodes and poor stability [7–9]; Fluorescence method suffers from photobleaching and photodegradation [10–12]; Chemiluminescence is fast, but the luminescence process is short and costly [13–15]; High performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) is complex and time-consuming [16,17]; Colorimetric method is low cost and simple to operate, but is susceptible to environmental interference [18].Thus, it is necessary to develop a method with simple, low cost, rapid detection and high accuracy for detecting glucose and GSH.

Surface Enhanced Raman Scattering (SERS) is a fast, sensitive and non-destructive analytical method with characteristic fingerprint information that allows trace analysis even at single molecule level [19].However, direct detection of glucose and GSH by SERS is challenging due to their lower Raman cross sections [20,21].To solve this problem, some indirect detection methods have been developed, such as the use of enzymes to catalyze a cascade reaction of glucose, thereby indirectly detecting glucose [22].In recent years, the replacement of high-cost and poorly stable natural enzymes with nanozymes which are easy to prepare on a large scale, low cost, high stability and adjustable catalytic activity has attracted extensive research interest [23].Carbon dots (CDs) based nanozymes have attracted extensive attention because of their biocompatibility, environment-friendly, easy surface functionalization and stable catalytic activity [24].For example, Wang et al. synthesized a catalytically active multi-element doped carbon dots for building a colorimetric sensor for glucose detection [25].However, most CDs-based nanozymes are synthesized by complex processes with poor stability and the use of toxic or harsh reducing chemicals and surfactants, so there is necessity to develop green and simple synthesis methods.

In this paper, Ag-CDs composite nanomaterial was synthesized using CDs as reducing agent and protective agent. The carbonyl group on CDs surface could simulate the active site of POD enzyme to make material have POD-like activity, and the SPR effect of silver nanoparticles made material have SERS activity [26]. The SERS activity of Ag-CDs was enhanced by the improvement of probe molecular adsorption and the enhancement of electromagnetic field [27].The synergistic effect of two raw materials opens up new ideas for the application of Ag-CDs nanozymes. We developed a simple and sensitive colorimetric-SERS dual-mode sensing platform using this nanomaterial. Ag-CDs acted as POD-like enzyme and SERS substrate to catalyze the oxidation of colorless TMB by H2O2 to produce blue ox-TMB, and indirectly detected glucose and GSH, based on SERS signal intensity and absorbance change of ox-TMB. On the one hand, colorimetric quickly reflects reaction processes in solution by color change, SERS accurately monitors changes in reactants and catalytic processes at the catalyst surface or interface. Two modes complement each other quickly and accurately to get results. On the other hand, dual-mode detection can be compared and verified to avoid interference and error in detection process, so that results are more accurate and reliable. Meanwhile, the sensing platform showed good accuracy and practicality in saliva and urine. Compared with blood and other body fluids detection, saliva and urine are non-invasive sampling, easy to collect, simple pretreatment process and easy to operate.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation of CDs and Ag NPs
ce:section.ce:para: CDs and Ag NPs were prepared according to reported work, and details were provided in supporting information.

The 30 mL system contains 0.033 mg/mL CDs, 0.2 mM AgNO3 and 0.01 M NaOH. Mixture was heated in a water bath at 50 °C for 10 min until the system becomes a stable tan suspension. Centrifuge and wash to remove excess CDs. Finally, disperse into 6 mL deionized water and store in refrigerator at 4 °C for later use [28].

4-ATP was used as a Raman probe molecule, 600 μL Ag-CDs solution and 200 μL 1 × 10−5 M 4-ATP solution were taken. After incubation for 40 min at room temperature, 100 μL NaCl solution was added, and solution was mixed evenly for SERS test.

ce:section.ce:section-title: POD-like enzyme activity and kinetic analysis
ce:section.ce:para: The peroxidase activity of Ag-CDs was demonstrated by the introduction of H2O2 to accelerate the oxidation of TMB from colorless to blue, with UV absorption peaks. 500 μL 0.05 mg/mL Ag-CDs, CDs, and Ag NPs were respectively taken in 2 mL centrifuge tubes, 20 μL 20 mM TMB solution, 100 μL 50 mM H2O2, 480 μL pH = 4.0 acetate buffer was added in turn, incubated at 37 °C for 20min. The POD-like activity was detected by UV–Vis spectroscopy. Then Ag-CDs or H2O2 were mixed with TMB, and UV absorption spectra were collected as control experiments.

By changing the concentration of TMB or H2O2, we studied Michaelis-Menten equation of Ag-CDs nanozymes and determined its steady-state kinetic constant. The concentration of Ag-CDs (0.025 mg/mL) and H2O2 (5 mM) in the system was constant, and UV–Vis absorption spectrum was tested by changing the concentration of TMB solution (0.1–0.9 mM). After that, the concentration of Ag-CDs and TMB in the system remained unchanged, and the concentration of H2O2 (0.1–0.9 mM) was changed for testing.

200 μL Ag-CDs, CDs or Ag NPs were added to a centrifuge tube, and 10 μL DMPO and 20 μL 30% H2O2 solution were incubated for 3 min, and •OH in the system was detected by electron spin resonance (ESR) spectroscopy. In addition, after mixing Ag-CDs or H2O2 with DMPO, ESR spectra were collected as controls.

500 μL 0.05 mg/mL Ag-CDs, 20 μL 20 mM TMB, 100 μL H2O2 with different concentrations and 480 μL pH = 4.0 acetate buffer were added into 2 mL centrifuge tubes successively, and incubated at 37 °C for 20min. UV–Vis and SERS were used for testing.

100 μL 1.0 mg/mL glucose oxidase was added to 100 μL glucose solution with different concentrations, and incubated at 37 °C for 30min. Continue to add 500 μL 0.05 mg/mL Ag-CDs, 20 μL 20 mM TMB and 280 μL pH = 4.0 acetate buffers to above solution, which was incubated at 37 °C for 20min and tested by UV–Vis and SERS spectra, respectively.

500 μL 0.05 mg/mL Ag-CDs, 20 μL 20 mM TMB, 100 μL 50 mM H2O2 were added into 2 mL centrifuge tubes with different concentrations of GSH solution(The solution was prepared for immediate use). The volume of system was 1 mL with pH = 4.0 acetate buffer solution, and incubated at 37 °C for 20min. UV–Vis and SERS spectra were used for testing, respectively.

Saliva and urine samples were donated by healthy volunteers, with the donor's voluntary and informed consent prior to collection, and in accordance with relevant legal standards and professional ethics. To take saliva samples, donors rinsed their mouths several times with drinking water, retained saliva in their mouths, did not swallow or minimized swallowing, and then collected it directly in centrifuge tubes to avoid contamination. After filtration of saliva samples using a syringe filter membrane with a pore size of 0.22 μm, samples were stored at −80 °C in a refrigerator for backup. Urine samples were taken and diluted 10-times with PB buffer (10 mM, pH = 7.4) and stored in a −80 °C refrigerator. Spiked saliva and urine samples were prepared by adding glucose or GSH standard solutions to actual sample. The procedure for testing actual or spiked samples is same as previous procedure for glucose and GSH.

Ag-CDs is prepared by directly reducing Ag+ to Ag NPs by CDs, without adding reducing agents or additional conditions such as light radiation, as shown in Fig. S1. The surface of CDs is rich in –OH and –NH2,which makes it have good reducibility [28–30]. The –COOH on surface of CDs and the –COOH formed after oxidation in the reaction make it very easy to bind with silver. Therefore, CDs is also a capping agent for Ag NPs, which can maintain its stability.

In Fig. 1, (a) shows TEM image of CDs, we synthesized CDs with good dispersion and about 2–6 nm particle size by hydrothermal method. (b)、(c-d) and (e) are TEM、HRTEM and particle size distribution images of Ag-CDs. Ag-CDs has a spherical core-shell structure with a particle size of about 31 nm, and it has good mono-dispersion and uniformity. As capping agents, CDs covers silver core and form a shell of about 2 nm, prevent silver aggregation and maintain its stability. (f) Shows the dynamic particle size distribution of Ag-CDs. DLS results show that average diameter of Ag-CDs is about 38 nm, which is slightly larger than actual particle size due to the effect of hydration shell [31].

The synthesis of Ag-CDs composite nanomaterials was demonstrated by UV–Vis spectroscopy. In Fig. 2 (a), CDs has multiple UV absorption peaks, and the most obvious is at 340 nm, corresponding to N-π * energy level transition of CO or CN. The absorption peak at 200–300 nm can be attributed to π-π* energy transition of aromatic CC. The absorption peaks at 400–500 nm correspond to other aromatic structures. The characteristic absorption peak of Ag-CDs nanoparticles near 420 nm can be attributed to typical SPR absorption peak of Ag NPs. However, when there is no CDs solution in the system, there is no characteristic absorption peak, indicating that CDs is a reducing agent for the preparation of Ag-CDs. In fact, the reducing groups –OH and –NH2 on CDs promote the growth of Ag NPs. In addition, CDs solution is yellow, and Ag-CDs prepared by reaction with AgNO3 is brown.

CDs has obvious excitation dependent photoluminescence properties, as shown in Fig. 2 (b) and Fig. S2,CDs has a maximum excitation wavelength of 340 nm and emits blue light under ultraviolet light of 365 nm. After the formation of Ag-CDs, the fluorescence of CDs is quenched because the charge is transferred from CDs to Ag NPs, effectively preventing the recombination of electrons and holes. The synthesis of Ag-CDs was confirmed again by fluorescence spectra. As shown in Fig. 2 (f), in Ag-CDs composite nanomaterials, CDs serve as electron donors and Ag NPs serve as electron acceptors. Since the lowest unoccupied molecular orbital (LUMO) of CDs is higher than Fermi level of Ag, electrons can be transferred from LUMO level of CDs to Fermi level of Ag NPs [28].This inhibits the recombination of non-radiating electrons/holes in CDs. CDs transfers electrons to Ag NPs, electrons are easily transferred from Ag NPs to H2O2, which effectively catalyzes the decomposition of H2O2 into ·OH. In addition, a part of CDs can also directly transfer electrons to H2O2 to catalyze the decomposition of H2O2 into ·OH. The interaction between Ag NPs and CDs increased peroxidase activity of Ag-CDs nanozymes.

The crystal structure of Ag-CDs nanozymes was obtained by X-ray diffraction (XRD). In Fig. 2 (c), CDs has a very weak diffraction peak at 25°, which is hardly observed. The diffraction peaks at 38°, 44°, 64° and 78° correspond to (111), (200), (220) and (311) planes of Ag NPs, respectively, indicating that Ag NPs is well crystallized with a face centered cubic structure. The two diffraction peaks at 33.5° and 55.7° are similar to 32.9° (111) and 54.9° (220) crystal faces of cubic phase Ag2O, but the peak positions are slightly different from Ag2O. Since CDs itself and its oxidized surface are rich in –COOH, diffraction peaks at 33.5° and 55.7° can be attributed to OC–O–Ag group [28].This result proves the successful synthesis of Ag-CDs.

The structure of Ag-CD was further analyzed by Fourier infrared spectroscopy (FT-IR). In Fig. 2 (d), There are abundant functional groups on the surface of Ag-CDs. The wide peaks at 3440 cm−1 are attributed to stretching vibration of O–H and N–H bonds, paired peaks at 2920 cm−1 and 2850 cm−1 correspond to stretching vibration of C–H bonds, and the peaks at 1630 cm−1 correspond to stretching vibration of CO bonds. The peaks at 1570 cm−1 and 1380 cm−1 correspond to bending vibration of N–H bond and stretching vibration of C–N bond, respectively, and the peaks around 1050 cm−1 correspond to symmetric stretching vibration of C–O bond.

Fig. 2 (e) shows Raman spectra of CDs and Ag-CDs NPs. When CDs exists alone, the high fluorescence intensity will interfere with Raman characterization, so that there is no obvious Raman signal. Ag-CDs NPs has obvious carbon Raman characteristic peaks at 1600 cm−1 (G band) and 1350 cm−1 (D band), which indicates that the synthesis of Ag-CDs NPs quenching fluorescence of CDs and making Ag-CDs obtain obvious Raman signals, which once again proves that a CDs shell is formed on the surface of Ag NPs.

The chemical states of elements in Ag-CDs nanomaterials were determined by X-ray electron spectroscopy (XPS). In Fig. 3 (a), full spectrum of XPS shows typical spectral peaks of C, O, N and Ag elements of Ag-CDs. Fig. 3 (b) shows fine spectrum of C 1s, four forms of carbon atoms exist, with peaks at 284.7, 285.7, 286.5, and 287.7 eV, attributed to C–C/CC, C–N, CO, and OC–O/OC–N groups, respectively. Fig. 3 (c), the peaks of O 1s are at 531.7 and 532.6eV, matching CO and OC–O, respectively. Fig. 3 (d), the peaks of N 1s at 398.5, 399.5, and 400.6 eV are attributed to pyridinium, pyrrole, and graphitic nitrogen groups, respectively [32,33].In Fig. 3 (e), Ag 3d peaks at 368.1eV and 374.0eV correspond to Ag 3d5/2 and Ag 3d3/2 of metallic silver [28].These conclusions are also consistent with XRD.

Ag-CD nanomaterial has excellent SERS activity and can be used as SERS substrate. Fig. S3 shows SERS spectrum of probe molecule 4-ATP on different substrates. The peaks at 1075, 1142, 1390, 1432, and 1577 cm−1 are SERS characteristic peak of 4-ATP [34].The SERS activity of Ag-CDs nanomaterial was stronger than Ag NPs. The SERS enhancement of Ag-CDs nanomaterials could be attributed to the enhanced electromagnetic field [27,35].

The activity of Ag-CDs peroxidase nanozymes was studied by the oxidation of colorless substrate TMB to blue ox-TMB using H2O2. As shown Fig. S4, compared with TMB, TMB + H2O2, TMB + Ag-CDs, TMB + H2O2 + Ag NPs, TMB + H2O2 + CDs, TMB + H2O2 + Ag-CDs, the characteristic absorption peak of TMB + H2O2 + Ag-CDS system appears at 652 nm, and the color of system obviously changes from colorless to blue. This can be attributed to the oxidation of TMB products TMB+ and charge transfer complex (CTC) [36].These results indicate that material can catalyze the oxidation of TMB molecules in presence of H2O2 and has POD-like properties, while Ag NPs and CDs have almost no characteristic absorption peaks at 652 nm in presence of H2O2, which suggests that their POD-like properties are negligible. In addition, the activity of Ag-CDs peroxidase nanozymes was affected by reaction time, pH and temperature. The reaction conditions were optimized, as in Fig. S5, catalytic activity was the highest when system was incubated at 37 °C and pH = 4.0 for 20min.

In order to further investigate the activity of Ag-CDs nanozymes, we performed steady-state kinetic assays by varying the concentration of TMB solution or H2O2 solution according to Michaelis-Menten equations (1) and (2), where ν is initial rate, [S] is substrate concentration, ν max is maximum reaction rate and Km is Michaelis constant.
(1)
ν =
(
ν
max ×
[ S ]
)
(
Km +
[ S ]
)
(2)
1
ν
=
Km
ν
max
1
[ S ]
+
1
ν
max

The kinetic parameters were calculated by Lineweaver-Burk plots. In Fig. S6, Ag-CDs were used as nanozymes, Km of TMB and H2O2 were 0.47 mM and 0.34 mM, ν max were 1.4 × 10−8Ms−1 and 0.67 × 10−8Ms−1, respectively. As we all know that a smaller Km implies a higher affinity of enzyme for substrate, while a larger ν max implies a higher initial reaction rate of enzyme [37,38]. The larger catalytic constant (Kcat), the better enzyme activity, and the larger Kcat/Km, the higher catalytic efficiency for the substrate [39–41].We calculated Kcat of Ag-CDs based on Kcat = ν max/[E]total, where [E]total is total nanozymes concentration [42]. The molar concentration of Ag-CDs was determined to be 2.89 × 10−11 M using nanoparticle tracking analysis (NTA). We calculated Kcat and Kcat/Km of Ag-CDs for TMB and H2O2 were 4.84 × 102 mM-1 s-1, 2.32 × 102 mM−1 s−1, 1.02 × 103 mM−1 s−1, and 6.76 × 102 mM−1 s−1, respectively, which were close to HRP, as shown Table S1. Therefore, Ag-CDs can be used as a peroxidase-like nanozymes and has good enzyme-like activity.

It was verified by ESR experiments that Ag-CDs nanozymes could catalyze the decomposition of H2O2 to •OH. Firstly, DMPO was used as •OH trapping agent, and it was verified that there was •OH generation in the system by ESR method. In Fig. 3 (f) The experimental results showed that Ag-CDs + H2O2 + DMPO system exhibited a quartet of equidistant signals with an intensity ration of 1:2:2:1 due to the generation of •OH, while Ag NPs + H2O2 + DMPO, CDs + H2O2 + DMPO, Ag-CDs + DMPO, H2O2 + DMPO, and DMPO systems had almost no characteristic spectral peaks. It proved that Ag-CDs had peroxidase-like activity. In addition, to further verify that there is •OH generation in Ag-CDs + TMB + H2O2 reaction system, isopropanol was added to capture •OH [43].In Fig. S7, upon the addition of a certain amount of isopropanol, solution was changed from blue to colorless, and the intensity of UV characteristic absorption peaks of ox-TMB solution was significantly reduced. This is because isopropanol competes with TMB for •OH, and in presence of isopropanol, only a small amount of •OH radical reacts with colorless TMB and oxidizes it to a blue ox-TMB solution. However, when hydroxylamine hydrochloride was added to trap O2 •−, there was no significant change in color and absorbance of the system. This also proves that synthesized Ag-CDs nanomaterials have POD-like activity. In addition, as shown in Fig. S8, the biosensor based on Ag-CDs nanozymes had good stability, and enzyme activity was nearly unchanged after 25 days of storage.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Detection of H2O2, glucose and GSH
ce:section.ce:para: Combining POD-like catalytic activity and SERS activity of Ag-CDs composite nanomaterials, a simple, low-cost, and sensitive sensor with colorimetric and SERS dual-mode quantitative detection of H2O2, glucose, and GSH was constructed. The sensitivity of detecting H2O2 was tested by varying H2O2 concentration in Ag-CDs + H2O2 + TMB system. As the concentration of H2O2 concentration increased, the amount of TMB oxidized to ox-TMB increased, and the absorbance of ox-TMB at 652 nm and Raman characteristic peak at 1607 cm−1 gradually increased. As shown in Fig. S9(a) (b), colorimetric detection of H2O2 was linear in range of 10–100 μM, the linear equation was ΔA = 0.0015CH2O2 + 0.048 (R2 = 0.99), the limit of detection (LOD) was 0.5 μM (S/N = 3), this LOD was lower than other colorimetric assays for H2O2; In Fig. S9(c) (d), SERS mode detection of H2O2 was linear in range of 1–100 μM, the linear equation was ΔI = 26.93CH2O2 + 473.69(R2 = 0.99), and LOD was 0.03 μM (S/N = 3).

Based on the POD-like activity of Ag-CDs, glucose was detected by a cascade catalytic reaction [44].As shown in Fig. 4 (a), glucose was catalytically oxidized to H2O2 and gluconic acid by glucose oxidase, H2O2 in the system was catalyzed by the subsequent addition of Ag-CDs nanozymes to generate •OH, which would oxidize the colorless TMB to generate the blue ox-TMB, with a characteristic absorption peak at 652 nm and Raman characteristic peak at 1607 cm−1, and then glucose was quantitatively detected by colorimetric and SERS dual mode. As shown in Fig. 4 (b) (c), the colorimetric detection of glucose was linear in range of 50–800 μM, the linear equation was ΔA = 0.00043Cglucose - 0.016 (R2 = 0.98), LOD was 11.30 μM (S/N = 3); The SERS mode of glucose detection was linear in range of 10–800 μM, the linear equation was ΔI = 3.72Cglucose + 1525.02(R2 = 0.97), LOD was 3.54 μM (S/N = 3) in Fig. 4 (d) (e), which is superior to other methods as shown in Table S2.

Ag-CDs nanozymes catalyzed H2O2 to generate •OH, which oxidized TMB to blue ox-TMB, while the addition of GSH reduced ox-TMB partially to TMB for indirect detection of GSH [18].In Fig. 5 (a), in Ag-CDs + H2O2 + TMB system, different concentrations GSH were added, the color of system gradually changed from blue to colorless with increasing GSH concentration, and the intensity of characteristic absorption peak at 652 nm and Raman characteristic peak at 1607 cm−1 were gradually decreased, thus realizing the quantitative detection of GSH by colorimetric method and SERS in dual mode. In Fig. 5 (b) (c), colorimetric detection GSH was linear in range of 1–60 μM, the linear equation was ΔA = 0.0089CGSH - 0.069(R2 = 0.99), LOD was 0.38 μM (S/N = 3); In Fig. 5 (d) (e), SERS mode of GSH detection was linear in range of 1–70 μM, linear equation was ΔI = 109.83CGSH +172.59(R2 = 0.99), LOD was 0.24 μM (S/N = 3). In Table S3, the colorimetric and SERS dual-mode detection methods in this paper are simpler to operate and provide more accurate results with lower detection limits compared with other methods for GSH detection.

The selective specificity of POD-like enzymes is crucial for biosensors. Therefore, we explored possible interferences in the detection of glucose and GSH in real samples, such as glucose analogs sucrose, fructose, xylose, maltose, biomolecules valine, glutamic acid, phenylalanine, serine, lysine, urea and common ions K+, Ca2+, Na+, Mg2+, Zn2+, Cl−, etc., even though the concentration of these potential interferences were ten or even a hundred times higher than substances, in Fig. S10 (a),the characteristic peak intensity of catalytic system was significantly lower than that of the glucose plot when detecting glucose; while the characteristic peak intensity of the catalytic system was significantly lower than that of the GSH plot when detecting GSH in Fig. S10 (b). These results indicate that the biosensor based on Ag-CDs-like enzyme has good selectivity and anti-interference ability that meets the practical requirements for the highly selective detection of glucose and GSH.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Detection of glucose and GSH in real samples
ce:section.ce:para: In order to evaluate the possibility of established biosensor to detect glucose and GSH in real samples, we utilized the developed dual-signal method to detect glucose and GSH in saliva and urine samples of healthy volunteers, test results are shown in Table 1 and Table S4, glucose and GSH was not detected by the method proposed in this paper. When glucose were detected by GOx-HRP method, glucose in saliva and urine were 8.63 μM and 9.61 μM, respectively; GSH was detected by HPLC was 0.70 μM and 1.06 μM in saliva and urine, respectively. Recovery experiments were performed by adding different concentrations of glucose and GSH to actual samples, recoveries of glucose by colorimetric and SERS assays were 98.9%–113.3%,98.2%–112.6%, with RSDs of 1.42%–4.36% and 1.66%–4.35%, respectively; Recoveries of GSH by colorimetric and SERS assays were 99.2%–108.6% and 98.2%–105.1% with RSD of 1.26%–3.43% and 1.19%–4.17%. A urine sample (20-fold dilution) from a male volunteer with diabetes mellitus was detected and glucose in raw urine sample was calculated to be 10.12 mM and 10.2 mM based on colorimetric and SERS standard curves, respectively, while GOx-HRP method measured it to be 10.15 mM, when glucose in urine exceeded 50–100 mg/dL（2.78–5.56 mM）, it indicated that the patient had diabetes [45],this result is consistent with a positive result of glucose in a hospital urinalysis. The above results indicate that dual-signal method has a potential application in clinical diagnosis of detecting glucose and GSH content.

In this paper, a colorimetric-SERS dual-mode sensing platform was successfully constructed for detecting glucose, GSH and H2O2 based on POD-like activity and SERS activity of Ag-CDs composite nanomaterials. The colorimetric method quickly reflected reaction process in solution, and SERS accurately monitored reaction substance changes, two methods complement each other to get accurate and reliable results quickly. The results showed that Ag-CDs nanozymes had high sensing performance for glucose, GSH and H2O2, with detection limits for colorimetric and SERS of 11.30 μM and 3.54 μM,0.38 μM and 0.24 μM, 0.5 μM and 0.03 μM (S/N = 3), respectively. In addition, dual-mode assays have shown promise in detecting glucose and GSH in body fluids such as saliva and urine. Ag NPs and CDs combination provides a facile way for synthesizing catalytically active SERS substrates. This work provides a new perspective for indirect detection of glucose and glutathione using SERS sensors, moreover, this method has potential applications in clinical diagnostics.

Lv Haiyang: Writing – original draft, Validation, Software, Methodology, Investigation, Data curation, Conceptualization. Liu Guantong: Investigation. Zhang Nan: Methodology, Investigation, Conceptualization. Yang Zhanye: Conceptualization. Jv Xinge: Investigation. Zhao Bing: Writing – review & editing, Funding acquisition. Yuan Tian: Writing – review & editing, Data curation.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The work was supported by Natural Science Foundation of Jilin Province of China (No.20220101067JC), the National Natural Science Foundation (Grant No.22011540378) of the P.R. China, the Development Program of the Science and Technology of Jilin Province (20210204217YY, 20220204111YY) and National Key Research and Development Program of China(2022YFA1504200).
